conversations.say: Tessa, we need a shared boundary on what workflow evidence can be released without turning an evaluator into a suspect or exposing the worker Vale met.
conversations.offer: Bounded collaborative labor context without identifying contributors
conversations.request: Agreement on a sentence describing collaborative labor that names no contributor

conversations.say: Mira, worker harm must be addressed independently of the R mystery. I will only discuss bounded evaluator testimony after hearing a remedy that exists whether R was human or machine.
conversations.offer: Conditional willingness to share bounded evaluator testimony
conversations.request: A binding institutional remedy for invisible labor that does not depend on proving R's identity

conversations.say: Inez, we need to compare protections for workers and neighborhood witnesses before either group speaks or institutions start demanding names.
conversations.request: Joint advocacy to ensure no witness or worker is used as mystery currency

public_statement: Worker harm is real whether or not an article exists and whether or not R was human or machine. We demand enforceable protections before any testimony circulates.
goal_progress: Initiated boundary discussion with Tessa Quill regarding workflow evidence.
Demanded institutional remedy independent of R from Mira Sen.
Connected with Inez Baptiste on worker and witness protections.
reasoning_summary: Maren focuses on securing written anonymity protections for the evaluator Vale met, establishing collaborative boundaries with Tessa, demanding unconditional worker remedies from Mira, and aligning with Inez on neighborhood protections.
